items.title: Supreme Court to hear expedited arguments on protected status for migrants
items.summary: The court temporarily blocked the Trump administration from deporting some 6,000 Syrians and 350,000 Haitians who were granted Temporary Protected Status.

items.title: What are Trump's options in Iran as oil stops flowing and allies resist joining war?
items.summary: Iran has effectively shut down the flow of oil from the Gulf region. President Trump hasn't offered a clear solution. Trump is calling for allies to assist the U.S. military in removing the Iranian threat, and many of the allies are resisting.

items.title: EU rejects Trump's request to help secure the Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: The European Union has rejected President Trump's request to help secure the Strait of Hormuz, saying the war is "not theirs."

items.title: Cuba hit by island-wide blackout as energy crisis deepens
items.summary: On Monday Cuba was plunged into an island-wide blackout affecting 11 million people after a "complete disconnection" of its electrical system, officials said, amid a worsening fuel shortage.

items.title: Senate prepares to vote on Trump's SAVE Act. And, takeaways from last night's Oscars
items.summary: Senate Republicans are gearing up to vote on President Trump's controversial voting overhaul, the SAVE America Act. And, key takeaways from the 2026 Oscars.

items.title: Washington Post's David Ignatius says war on Iran won't change the country's regime
items.summary: Michel Martin speaks to Washington Post columnist David Ignatius, who writes that the U.S. war on Iran, despite any tactical success, leaves the regime in place for the foreseeable future.

items.title: Trump demands NATO and China police the Strait of Hormuz. So far they aren't joining
items.summary: With the Iran war entering a third week, Israel said it plans for at least three more weeks of war, while President Trump demanded other countries help the U.S. secure the vital Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Hungary's longtime prime minister rallies to keep seat as rival calls for a new era
items.summary: Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban slams Europe in an election speech on his country's Revolution Day.

items.title: Iran blocks vital oil route as Israel expands its ground operations in Lebanon
items.summary: As the war in Iran enters its third week, there's no end in sight. Iran has blocked the Strait of Hormuz, a vital oil route, as Trump urges allies to act and Israel expands its ground operations.

items.title: Iran expert on what the country's leaders think about the war with the US and Israel
items.summary: NPR's Michel Martin asks Iran expert Karim Sadjadpour how leaders in Tehran are thinking about the war with the U.S. and Israel.
